aliases: U.S. Military Academy at West Point
United States Military Academy at West Point
description: federal service academy in West Point, New York, United States
description_wiki: The United States Military Academy (USMA), commonly known as West Point, is a United States service academy in West Point, New York, that educates cadets for service as commissioned officers in the United States Army.

description: U.S. Navy federal service academy in the Annapolis, Maryland area
description_wiki: The United States Naval Academy (USNA, Navy, or Annapolis) is a federal service academy adjacent to Annapolis, Maryland.

aliases: Revenue Cutter Service School of Instruction
description: U.S. federal service academy for the Coast Guard
description_wiki: The United States Coast Guard Academy (USCGA), located in New London, Connecticut, is the U.S. service academy specifically for the United States Coast Guard.

description: United States Army post in Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Fort Knox is a United States Army installation in Kentucky, south of Louisville and north of Elizabethtown.

surface_forms: Third Infantry Division was the first European division
description: formation of the United States Army (since 1917)
description_wiki: The 3rd Infantry Division (3ID) (nicknamed Rock of the Marne) is a combined arms division of the United States Army based at Fort Stewart, Georgia.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: 1947–1991 tension between the Soviet Union and the United States and their respective allies
description_wiki: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc.

description: main battle tank family
description_wiki: The M1 Abrams () is a third-generation American main battle tank designed by Chrysler Defense (now General Dynamics Land Systems) and named for General Creighton Abrams.

description: region in East Asia
description_wiki: Korea is a peninsular region in East Asia consisting of the Korean Peninsula, Jeju Island, and smaller islands.

description: 1990–1991 conflict between Iraq and a 42-country coalition
description_wiki: The Gulf War was an armed conflict between Iraq and a 42-country coalition led by the United States.

aliases: The University of Texas at Austin
description: public university in Austin, Texas, USA
description_wiki: The University of Texas at Austin (UT Austin, UT, or Texas) is a public research university in Austin, Texas, United States.

description: military unit
description_wiki: The United States Army Acquisition Corps (AAC) is the officer / NCO corps of the United States Army  Acquisition Workforce (AAW), a branch which includes civilians, officers, and NCOs.

description: U.S. military unit
description_wiki: The 21st Theater Sustainment Command (21 TSC) provides theater sustainment throughout EUCOM and AFRICOM Areas of Responsibility in support of USAREUR and 7th Army.

description: city in the region of Franconia, Northern Bavaria, Germany
description_wiki: Würzburg (German: [ˈvʏʁtsbʊʁk] ; Main-Franconian: Wörtzburch, historical engl.

description: country in Southeast Europe
description_wiki: Bosnia and Herzegovina, often referred to as Bosnia-Herzegovina or simply as Bosnia, is a country in Southeast Europe.

description: multinational peacekeeping deployment to Bosnia and Herzegovina led by NATO
description_wiki: The Implementation Force (IFOR) was a NATO-led multinational peace enforcement force in Bosnia and Herzegovina under a one-year mandate from 20 December 1995 to 20 December 1996 under the codename Operation Joint Endeavour.

description: former peacekeeping force of the NATO
division of the United States Army
description_wiki: The 1st Armored Division, nicknamed "Old Ironsides", is a combined arms division of the United States Army.

description: sovereign state in Western Asia
description_wiki: Kuwait, officially the State of Kuwait, is a country in West Asia.

description: Material Command of the United States Army
description_wiki: The United States Army Materiel Command (AMC) is the primary provider of materiel to the United States Army via its contracting support brigades.

aliases: Gulf War 2/battle of Ramadi/battle of Faluya
description: 2003–2011 war after an American-led invasion
global international and intergovernmental organization
description_wiki: The United Nations (UN) is a global intergovernmental organization established by the signing of the UN Charter on 26 June 1945 with the articulated mission of maintaining international peace and security, to develop friendly relations among states, to promote international cooperation, and to serve as a centre for harmonizing the actions of states in achieving those goals.

surface_forms: Defense Supply Center Philadelphia European Region
description: government agency
description_wiki: The Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) is a combat support agency in the United States Department of Defense.

description: country in the Persian Gulf
description_wiki: Bahrain, officially the Kingdom of Bahrain, is an island country in West Asia.

description: country in Western Asia
description_wiki: The United Arab Emirates (UAE), also known simply as the Emirates, is a country in West Asia, situated at the eastern end of the Arabian Peninsula.

description: country in Central and South Asia
description_wiki: Afghanistan, officially the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan, is a landlocked country located at the crossroads of Central and South Asia.

description: English-language international newspaper
description_wiki: The International Herald Tribune (IHT) was a daily English-language newspaper published in Paris, France, for international English-speaking readers.

aliases: The Wall Street journal. Eastern edition
description: American business-focused daily newspaper
description_wiki: The Wall Street Journal (WSJ), commonly known as the Journal, is an American newspaper based in Midtown Manhattan, New York City.
